content.description:
A runtime debugging library that identifies which item and view is responsible for any rendered element on screen.

The primary UI is the [Element Inspector](item://eeee0000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002) item. This library provides the underlying inspection engine.

## API

```js
const inspector = api.require('element-inspector');
```

| Export | Signature | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| `inspectOnce` | `(api) → Promise<info\|null>` | Enter inspect mode, wait for one click, return element info. Resolves `null` on Escape. |
| `collectElementInfo` | `(element) → info` | Walk up from element collecting data-* attribution into a chain |
| `resolveAndNavigate` | `(api, {id, name, line}) → Promise` | Resolve an inspector link and open the target item as a sibling on the canvas |
| `activate` | `(api) → state` | Start persistent inspect mode (for REPL use). Returns `{ toggle(), isActive() }` |
| `deactivate` | `() → void` | Remove all event listeners and clean up |
| `inspect` | `(element, api) → info` | Manually inspect a DOM element and log to console (for REPL use) |

## How It Works

1. The rendering system sets `data-item-id`, `data-view-id`, `data-source`, and `data-source-line` attributes on rendered elements (when debug mode is active)
2. `inspectOnce` enters crosshair mode — hover highlights elements, click collects info
3. `collectElementInfo` walks up the DOM tree collecting attribution from `data-*` attributes
4. `resolveAndNavigate` resolves item references and opens them as siblings on the spatial canvas

## See Also

- [Element Inspector](item://eeee0000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002) — The inspector item (primary UI)
- [element-inspector (type)](item://eeee0000-0000-0000-0000-000000000010) — Type definition
- [viewport-rendering](item://f1111111-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — Sets data attributes during render
- [REPL](item://f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — Alternative debugging via the console

inspectOnce.name: inspectOnce
inspectOnce.description:
Enter inspect mode, wait for one click, return collected info.
Resolves with element info on click, or null if cancelled (Escape).

resolveAndNavigate.name: resolveAndNavigate
resolveAndNavigate.description:
Resolve an inspector link target and navigate to it.
Opens as sibling on spatial canvas if available.
